title: Conflict between the Constantinopolitans and Alexandrians on Account of Heraclides; Flight of Theophilus and the Bishops of his Party.

source.title: NPNF2-02. Socrates and Sozomenus Ecclesiastical Histories

verses.book: The Ecclesiastical History of Socrates Scholasticus.
verses.text: In the first place, then, Theophilus attempted to investigate the case of the ordination of Heraclides, that thereby he might if possible find occasion of again deposing John.

verses.book: The Ecclesiastical History of Socrates Scholasticus.
verses.text: Heraclides was not present at this scrutiny.

verses.book: The Ecclesiastical History of Socrates Scholasticus.
verses.text: He was nevertheless judged in his absence, on the charge of having unjustly beaten some persons, and afterwards dragged them in chains through the midst of the city of Ephesus.

verses.book: The Ecclesiastical History of Socrates Scholasticus.
verses.text: As John and his adherents remonstrated against the injustice of passing sentence upon an absent person, the Alexandrians contended that they ought to hear the accusers of Heraclides, although he was not present.

verses.book: The Ecclesiastical History of Socrates Scholasticus.
verses.text: A sharp contest therefore ensued between the Alexandrians and the Constantinopolitans, and a riot arose whereby many persons were wounded, and some were killed.

verses.book: The Ecclesiastical History of Socrates Scholasticus.
verses.text: Theophilus, seeing what was done, fled to Alexandria without ceremony; and the other bishops, except the few who supported John, followed his example, and returned to their respective sees.

verses.book: The Ecclesiastical History of Socrates Scholasticus.
verses.text: After these transactions, Theophilus was degraded, in every one’s estimation: but the odium attached to him was exceedingly increased by the shameless way in which he continued to read Origen’s works.

verses.book: The Ecclesiastical History of Socrates Scholasticus.
verses.text: And when he was asked why he thus countenanced what he had publicly condemned, he replied, ‘Origen’s books are like a meadow enameled with flowers of every kind.

verses.book: The Ecclesiastical History of Socrates Scholasticus.
verses.text: If, therefore, I chance to find a beautiful one among them, I cull it: but whatever appears to me to be thorny, I step over, as that which would prick.’

verses.book: The Ecclesiastical History of Socrates Scholasticus.
verses.text: But Theophilus gave this answer without reflecting on the saying of the wise Solomon, that ‘the words of the wise are as goads’; and those who are pricked by the precepts they contain, ought not to kick against them.

verses.book: The Ecclesiastical History of Socrates Scholasticus.
verses.text: For these reasons then Theophilus was held in contempt by all men.

verses.book: The Ecclesiastical History of Socrates Scholasticus.
verses.text: Dioscorus bishop of Hermopolis, one of those termed ‘the Tall Monks,’ died a short time after the flight of Theophilus, and was honored with a magnificent funeral, being interred in the church at ‘The Oak,’ where the Synod was convened on John’s account.

verses.book: The Ecclesiastical History of Socrates Scholasticus.
verses.text: John meanwhile was sedulously employed in preaching.

verses.book: The Ecclesiastical History of Socrates Scholasticus.
verses.text: He ordained Serapion bishop of Heraclea in Thrace, on whose account the odium against himself had been raised.

verses.book: The Ecclesiastical History of Socrates Scholasticus.
verses.text: Not long after the following events occurred.
